repo_prior_art.cite: flagship_explore_f1: full-history/coarsened feasible-set comparison is adjacent but targets a T=3 sustained-regime LP, not this two-stage memory splitter

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024: generic automated discrete sharp-bounds machinery; plus generic coarsening/refinement monotonicity

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_late_bounded_defiers: one-period collapsed Balke-Pearl complier-LATE envelope; no post-baseline assignment-memory splitter

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 static binary-IV ATE bounds and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 generic discrete bounds do not state the joint mixture-subtraction face

repo_prior_art.cite: flagship_explore_f1: dynamic full-history T=3 sustained-regime separation, not a two-stage pure-never-taker memory-gain diagnostic

published_prior_art.cite: SojitraSyrgkanis2024 and MichaelCuiLorchTchetgen2024 give dynamic point/model identification under stronger restrictions, not nonparametric strict memory-bounds gain
SwansonHernanMillerRobinsRichardson2018: static binary-IV covariate standardization; dynamic memory-cell complier weighting is not stated

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 is described as a generic finite-LP/linear-programming framework, but the verified paper presents automated polynomial-programming bounds with dual relaxation and branch-and-bound; the comparison should be corrected while preserving the generic-computation contrast.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter checklist gives only two close dynamic prior-art candidates for the strict-gain statement and omits ChenZhang2023/JRSSB, a closer time-varying-IV partial-identification/Bellman comparator that the proposal itself cites.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct the Duarte et al. comparison to polynomial-programming/Autobounds language and fix the bibliography metadata for Noam Finkelstein, volume 119 issue 547 pages 1778-1793.
Add ChenZhang2023 to the named checklist for Conjecture 2 and explicitly state why Bellman partial-identification for IV-optimal regimes does not subsume the pure-never-taker memory-gain diagnostic.
Promote the strict-gain conjecture with a one-cell rational witness specification: give qC, qN, e2 values, and observed conditional means whose full-memory and coarsened LP endpoints differ.
Sharpen Conjecture 3 by stating endpoint formulas for the weighted Minkowski sum and the exact condition under which cross-X compatibility is absent.

recommended_next_step: Patch the literature comparison/checklist issues, then keep the kernel; no repo or published prior art appears to subsume the main mixture-splitting conjectures.
